skill.name: n8n-mcp-tools-expert
skill.description: Guide for selecting and using n8n-mcp tools to search nodes, validate configurations, manage workflows, and deploy templates.
skill.summary: Use n8n-mcp tools to build, validate, and manage n8n workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed file is a Markdown guide with inline tool names and JavaScript-style examples. No prompt injection, malicious command execution, credential exfiltration, or hidden network behavior was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill guides users toward n8n API operations that require credentials and can change workflows.

low_findings.title: External command detections are documentation examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged backticked tool names and JavaScript snippets as Ruby or shell backticks. These lines document n8n-mcp tool calls and do not contain executable shell code, command interpolation, or installation scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown tables or fenced examples for MCP tool calls. No shell interpreter, Ruby code, or user-controlled command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL is source metadata
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the upstream repository in the skill front matter. It is not used by executable code and does not create a runtime network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in metadata at the top of the Markdown file. There is no code path that fetches or posts data to it.

low_findings.title: API credential reference is operational documentation
low_findings.description: The skill states that some n8n-mcp tools require N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY. This is not a secret value or direct environment access, but it is a real operational boundary because those tools can create, update, test, deploy, and activate workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential names are documented requirements, not leaked values. The listed API-backed operations are powerful enough to warrant a low-risk marketplace warning.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography detections are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to ordinary documentation text, node names, timing notes, and example parameters. No cryptographic library, hashing function, cipher mode, or password handling logic appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found a Markdown guide rather than code that implements cryptography. The repeated detections appear to come from substring matches in prose and examples.

low_findings.title: Critical dangerous-combination heuristic is dismissed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined static signals for command execution, network access, and credentials. Manual review found no executable code, no data exfiltration path, and no prompt injection attempt; the file is user-facing n8n-mcp guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The heuristic depends on lower-level findings that are mostly false positives. The only real concern is documented use of n8n API credentials for legitimate workflow management.

content.user_title: Build n8n Workflows with MCP Tools
content.value_statement: n8n-mcp has many tools, formats, and validation modes that are easy to misuse. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical guide for selecting tools, validating nodes, and managing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains which n8n-mcp tool to use for node search, node details, validation, templates, and workflow management.
Documents the two node type formats used by search and workflow tools.
Shows validation workflows for node configuration and full workflow checks.
Describes template search, template inspection, and template deployment patterns.
Lists n8n API-backed tools that require N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY.
Summarizes common mistakes, performance expectations, and recommended update patterns.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not an MCP server implementation.
It does not include the referenced SEARCH_GUIDE, VALIDATION_GUIDE, or WORKFLOW_GUIDE files.
API-backed operations require a configured n8n-mcp environment and valid n8n credentials.
It cannot verify live workflow behavior without access to the target n8n instance.

use_cases.title: Build an automation workflow
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose the right discovery, validation, creation, update, and activation tools for an n8n workflow.

use_cases.title: Validate node configuration
use_cases.description: Use the validation guidance to check required fields, runtime settings, and common configuration mistakes before deployment.

use_cases.title: Deploy from templates
use_cases.description: Use template search and deployment guidance to find reusable workflows and understand required credentials before rollout.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Node
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the n8n-mcp tools guide to find the best node for sending Slack messages. Show the search tool, node type, and next validation step.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Node
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to validate this n8n node configuration. Identify the correct validation mode, profile, and likely fixes before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Workflow Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the n8n-mcp workflow guidance to plan a webhook-to-HTTP-to-Slack workflow. Include discovery, creation, validation, update, and activation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to update an existing n8n workflow through partial operations. Include smart parameters, validation checkpoints, and safe activation guidance.

output_examples.input: Find the correct n8n-mcp tool flow for adding Slack to a workflow.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends searching for Slack nodes, retrieving standard node details, validating required fields, then adding the workflow node with the workflow node type format.

output_examples.input: Validate a workflow before enabling it in production.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines runtime validation, fixes missing fields, checks workflow connections, then recommends activation only after validation returns no blocking errors.

output_examples.input: Use an n8n template for webhook processing.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests searching templates by task, inspecting structure first, reviewing required credentials, and deploying only after confirming the target n8n instance.

best_practices: Use standard node detail before requesting full schemas to reduce token usage.
Validate after each significant workflow change and before activation.
Confirm credential requirements before using API-backed deployment or execution tools.
anti_patterns: Do not mix search node type formats with workflow node type formats.
Do not activate API-managed workflows before validation and credential review.
Do not request full node schemas when documentation or property search is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill run n8n workflows by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI assistant in using n8n-mcp tools. Live operations require the MCP server and valid n8n access.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why are there two node type formats?
faq.answer: Search and validation tools use short formats. Workflow creation and update tools use full n8n node package formats.

faq.question: Can it help with templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents template search, template detail review, and deployment patterns for n8n-mcp.

faq.question: Does it require API credentials?
faq.answer: Discovery and validation can work without n8n API access. Workflow management tools require N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: API-backed tools can modify or activate workflows. Users should validate workflows and protect n8n credentials.
